plang_failures_note: 203 excludes the 4 intentional fail-fixtures (Tests/{TestModule,Modules/Test}/Report/_fixtures_fail + _fixtures_sensitive). The single 'Failed to deserialize List`1' stdout line is a negative-path mock test feeding bad JSON, not a failure. Both unchanged from v4 baseline; the app-lowercase merge left the suites green.
scope: Re-review of coder v6 (894d6a0ca) — auditor F-A fix (persistent-grant durability). F-B (app-lowercase merge) is a mechanical rename already cleared by codeanalyzer v4; both suites re-run to confirm it left them green.

mutation_verification.mutation: permission/this.cs:147 SkipFreshnessCheck true -> false, clean rebuild, full C# suite
mutation_verification.assessment: The flag neutralises TWO checks (Ed25519 step 2 wire-freshness, step 4 nonce-replay). The mutation flips both; only the step-2 half is caught. coder v6's commit claims 'Mutation-verified' of the fix — literally true but the step-4 half has no test behind it.

findings.title: Nonce-replay half of the F-A fix is ungated
findings.detail: Persisted Find re-deserializes a fresh Data each call (SettingsStore.GetAll), so the per-instance VerifiedFlag cache does not carry across reads; two Finds = two VerifySignature passes. With step 4 active the second pass hits NonceReplay -> re-prompt. No test does two verifications of a persisted grant (Scenario3's read 1 creates without verifying; Scenario4 variants verify once in app2). A step-4-only revert passes all 2854 tests. Confirmed by a scratch probe: app2 reads twice -> re-prompts (Type 'ask') under mutation, covered with the fix. Exact closing test in tester/v5/result.md, mutation-verified.

non_blocking_notes.detail: ValidatePathTests.UpperCasedRootPrefix_..._OnUnix docstring still over-claims the RootComparison gate (carried from tester v4 N1).

auditor F-5 (attributed to tester): MoveCopyBundledConsentTests covers bundled consent only on the v2 Path surface; real copy.cs/move.cs handlers issue two prompts. Add a note or handler-path test. Fair to defer with F-C/D/E.

verdict_note: coder v6's F-A fix is correct and the suite is green, but the F-A regression guard covers only half the mechanism. F1 (major): the nonce-replay half is ungated — a plausible regression survives the full suite. One added test (handed over, mutation-verified) closes it. Re-issue as a quick v7.
summary: Clean rebuild: C# 2854/2854, PLang 203/203 — app-lowercase merge left suites green. coder v6's SkipFreshnessCheck fix for auditor F-A is correct (verified working by mutation), but the flag neutralises two independent checks and only step 2 (wire-freshness) has a test. F1: step 4 (nonce-replay) skip is ungated — no test verifies a persisted grant twice, so a step-4-only revert passes all 2854 tests and would silently re-break 'always allow' for repeated foreign-resource reads. Verdict: needs-work, one major finding with the closing test handed over.
